# 3 hospitalized in Chesterfield shopping center crash

**TL;DR:** A vehicle crashed into a shopping center on Midlothian Turnpike in Chesterfield County on June 8, injuring three people.

## Quick facts

- **Who:** Three adults
- **What:** Vehicle crashed into shopping center building and two parked vehicles
- **When:** Monday, June 8, 2026 evening
- **Where:** 13100 block of Midlothian Turnpike, Chesterfield County

## The story

A vehicle crashed into a shopping center at 13100 Midlothian Turnpike in Chesterfield County on the evening of Monday, June 8, striking the building as well as two other vehicles in the parking area. Three adults were injured in the collision and were all transported to local hospitals with minor injuries.

Chesterfield County Police Department officers and emergency responders arrived at the scene to assist the injured and document the incident. Visible damage was reported to a brick structural support outside what appears to be a chiropractor's office within the shopping center complex.

The cause of the crash was under investigation by the Chesterfield County Police Department at the time of initial reporting. Police did not immediately provide information about whether the crash involved driver error, a medical emergency, or mechanical failure.

**Why it matters:** The incident highlights ongoing safety concerns at shopping centers where parked vehicles and pedestrians are vulnerable to uncontrolled vehicle impacts. Emergency responders' quick response ensured all three injured individuals received prompt medical care.

- [Midlothian Turnpike](https://www.openstreetmap.org/search?query=Midlothian+Turnpike+Chesterfield+VA)

- **2026-06-08** — Vehicle crashes into Chesterfield shopping center: A vehicle struck the building and two other vehicles at 13100 Midlothian Turnpike.
